uk: Неділя 29-та по Зісланні Святого Духа, святих Праотців. Глас 4. Єв. 7.
🕂 Свв. мучеників Євстратія, Авксентія, Євгенія, Мардарія й Ореста
saints.en: 28th Sunday after Pentecost. Sts. Martyrs Eustratius, Auxentius, Eugene, Mardarius & Orestes. St. Martyr Lucy the Virgin

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» – 10 стихир: 3 воскрес., 3 мчч. і 4 Праотців, Слава: Праотців, І нині: богородичний-догмат 4-го гласу. Читань немає. Стиховня воскресна, Слава: Праотців, І нині: богород. По «Отче наш»: тропар воскресний, Слава: Праотців, І нині: богородичний за голосом наславника. Відпуст великий недільний.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар недільний (2), Слава: мчч., І нині: Праотців. Катизми чергові, і сідальні недільні. Полієлей, величання мчч. «Ангельський собор», іпакой і всі три сідальних мчч. Степенна 4-го гласу. Прокімен воскресний. Єв. – Ів. зач. 63; 20, 1-10. «Воскресіння Христове». Псалом 50. Стихира недільна. Канон воскресний, мчч. і Праотців. Катавасія: «Христос народжується». По 3-ій пісні – кондак-ікос і сідальний мчч., Слава, і нині: іпакой Праотців; по 6-ій – кондак-ікос Праотців; по 9-ій – «Свят Господь Бог наш», воскресний світильний, Слава: мчч., І нині: богородичний Праотців. Хвалитних стихир – 8: 3 воскресних, 2 Праотців і З мчч., Слава: Праотців, І нині: «Преблагословенна». По славослов'ї – тропар недільний «Воскресши з гробу». Відпуст великий з поминанням недільним. По відпусті – Слава, І нині: євангельська стихира.
Часи: на всіх: тропар воскресний, також Слава: на 1-му і 6-му – Праотців, на 3-му і 9-му – мчч. Кондаки Праотцям і мчч. – поперемінно, тобто: на 1-му і 6-му – Праотцям, на 3-му і 9-му – мчч.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний, Праотцям і мчч.; Слава: кондак мчч., І нині: Праотців. Прокімен, апостол, алилуя і євангелія – спочатку Праотцям, також мученикам. Неділі: Ап. – Кол. 257 зач.; 3, 4-11. Єв. – Лк. 76 зач.; 14, 16-24. Мчч.: Ап. – Еф. 233 зач.; 6, 10-17. Єв. – Лк. 106 зач.; 21, 12-19. Причасний «Хваліте» і мученикам.

troparion_en.text: By faith You justified the Forefathers,  /  when through them You betrothed Yourself beforehand to the Church of the Gentiles.  /  The saints boast in glory,  /  that from their seed there is a glorious fruit:  /  she who bore You without seed.  /  By their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion_en.text:
(The Original Melody)
You did not worship the graven image,  /  O thrice-blessed youths,  /  but shielded by the ineffable
Essence,  /  you were glorified in your trial by fire.  /  In the midst of the unbearable fire you called
upon God, crying:  /  "Hasten, O compassionate One.  /  and in Your mercy, come to our aid,  /  for You
can do so if You will."1

troparion.text: By faith You justified the Forefathers,  /  when through them You betrothed Yourself beforehand to the Church of the Gentiles.  /  The saints boast in glory,  /  that from their seed there is a glorious fruit:  /  she who bore You without seed.  /  By their prayers, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(The Original Melody)
You did not worship the graven image,  /  O thrice-blessed youths,  /  but shielded by the ineffable
Essence,  /  you were glorified in your trial by fire.  /  In the midst of the unbearable fire you called

can do so if You will."1

oca_saints.name: Repose of Venerable Herman of Alaska, Wonderworker of All America

troparion.text: O blessed Father Herman of Alaska, / north star of Christ’s holy Church, / the light of your holy life and great deeds / guides those who follow the Orthodox way. / Together we lift high the Holy Cross / you planted firmly in America. / Let all behold and glorify Jesus Christ, / singing his holy Resurrection.

kontakion.text: The eternal light of Christ our Savior, / guided you, O blessed father Herman, / on your evangelical journey to America, / proclaiming the Gospel of peace. / Now you stand before the throne of Glory; / intercede for your land and its people / peace for the world and salvation for our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Eustratius, Auxentius, Eugene, Mardarius, and Orestes, at Sebaste

troparion.text: O radiance of the Martyrs revered by all,  / we glorify the five Passion-bearers,  / for despising earthly glory,  /  O most luminous sun and most wise orator Eustratios,  / with the other Martyrs you willingly endured fire and torments for Christ, the King of all;  / and from His throne of glory He crowned you with crowns of honor.  /  By their intercessions, O Christ God, save our souls.

kontakion.text:
[Podoben: “Neither the tomb, nor death could hold…” (Kontakion for the Dormition)]
You shone as a most brilliant light for those who sat in the darkness of ignorance, O Prizewinner.  /  And armed with faith as with a spear,  / you were not frightened by the audacity of your adversaries,  / O Eustratios, most eloquent of orators.

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Come, let us worship the Word...”)
Wearing the radiant mantle of virginity,  /  and virginally betrothed to the Life-giver Christ, /  you forsook the love of your earthly betrothed; /  therefore, as a bridal gift,  /  you brought to Him the outpouring of your blood.  /  O Virgin Martyr Lucy, intercede with Him for us all!

kontakion.text: We honor you, pure Virgin and trophy-bearer Lucy,  / and we praise your illustrious contest;  / for you desired only the beauty of Christ,  / disdaining all corruptible things.  / When you were beheaded, you received incorruptible glory.

troparion.text: Guided by your spiritual mentor, the Venerable Father Ephraim, an excellent zealot of obedience,  / O Father Arcadius, you fled from worldly vanity and were a superb monk;  / working diligently, you have received a crown of heavenly glory from Christ God. /  And now, together with the Venerable Father Ephraim, /  pray for us before the Throne of the Most High,  / that we may be delivered from all troubles and sorrows,  / and for the salvation of our souls.

kontakion.text: By directing your mind to the depths of the knowledge of God,  / you ascended to the heavenly abodes,  / and your life was equal to that of an angel, O Father Arcadius.  / You were obedient to your spiritual guide and teacher, the Venerable Ephraim, in all things.  / And now, standing with him before the Throne of the Master Christ,  / pray unceasingly for all of us.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Mardarios the Recluse of the Kiev Caves

troparion.text: O blessed Mardários, for the sake of Christ you possessed nothing,  / and because of your many good works, He dwelt in your heart.  / Pray for us who praise you with hymns.

kontakion.text: O glorious Mardários,  / you aspired to heavenly things, coveting nothing earthly,  /  and through non-acquisitiveness you became a chariot of fire ascending to Heaven,  / and were found worthy to dwell with the Angels.  / Now with them, pray unceasingly to Christ God for all of us.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Arsenius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles,  /  you occupied their throne,  /  and through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O divinely-inspired one;  /  Therefore, by rightly dividing the word of truth, you suffered for the Faith,  /  even unto the shedding of your blood.  /  O Hieromartyr Gabriel, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You were lifted up…”)
As one who lived piously among Hierarchs and who underwent martyrdom,  / O divinely-wise one, you abolished the sacrifices of idolatry and showed yourself as a protector of your flock.  / Therefore, we cry fervently to you:  /  “By your intercessions, deliver us from all misfortunes,  / O Gabriel our Father.”
